speaker: SENTRY
text: If we be not relieved within this hour,

speaker: SENTRY
text: We must return to th’ court of guard. The night

speaker: SENTRY
text: Is shiny, and they say we shall embattle

speaker: SENTRY
text: By th’ second hour i’ th’ morn.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: This last day was a shrewd one to ’s.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: O, bear me witness, night—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: What man is this?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Stand close, and list him.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Be witness to me, O thou blessèd moon,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: When men revolted shall upon record

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Bear hateful memory, poor Enobarbus did

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Before thy face repent.

speaker: SENTRY
text: Enobarbus?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Peace! Hark further.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: O sovereign mistress of true melancholy,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The poisonous damp of night dispunge upon me,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: That life, a very rebel to my will,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: May hang no longer on me. Throw my heart

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Against the flint and hardness of my fault,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Which, being dried with grief, will break to powder

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And finish all foul thoughts. O Antony,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Nobler than my revolt is infamous,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Forgive me in thine own particular,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But let the world rank me in register

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A master-leaver and a fugitive.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: O Antony! O Antony!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Let’s speak to him.

speaker: SENTRY
text: Let’s hear him, for the things he speaks may

speaker: SENTRY
text: concern Caesar.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Let’s do so. But he sleeps.

speaker: SENTRY
text: Swoons rather, for so bad a prayer as his

speaker: SENTRY
text: Was never yet for sleep.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Go we to him.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Awake, sir, awake! Speak to us.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Hear you, sir?

speaker: SENTRY
text: The hand of death hath raught him.

speaker: SENTRY
text: Hark, the drums

speaker: SENTRY
text: Demurely wake the sleepers. Let us bear him

speaker: SENTRY
text: To th’ court of guard; he is of note. Our hour

speaker: SENTRY
text: Is fully out.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Come on then. He may recover yet.
